findings.reasoning: This page is a discount/offer page for TurboTax Expert Full Service, Expert Assist, and Do It Yourself products, promoting a 15% off discount for expert-assisted filing. Scanning the full text, there is no mention of 'TurboTax Free', 'Free Edition', or any $0 filing offer in the body content. The only occurrence of 'free' is in the phrase '100% free to start. Pay only when we file,' which refers to the general TurboTax Expert Full Service payment model (pay only when you file), not the TurboTax Free Edition product. There is also a footer link labeled 'Free Edition tax filing' which is just a navigation link to another page, not a mention/promotion of the free product on this page itself. Since the rule's trigger is 'if TurboTax free is mentioned' referring to the free product/offer, and no such product mention appears in the substantive page content (only a generic footer navigational link list), the rule is not triggered. Counter-reading: one could argue the footer link 'Free Edition tax filing' constitutes a 'mention' of TurboTax Free, requiring the disclosure right underneath it. However, this is a standard site-wide navigation footer link list, not a promotional mention of the free product with specific claims requiring disclosure -- it's structurally identical to how a footer would list any product page link (Deluxe, Premium, etc.) without triggering disclosure requirements for each. Also '100% free to start' describes the Full Service expert product's payment structure (free until you decide to file), not the TurboTax Free Edition product covered by the D-01 disclosure. Given the ambiguity is low (footer nav links are not typically considered product 'mentions' triggering disclosure rules), I conclude trigger not met.

Scanned the full page text for any mention of "TurboTax Free," "Free Edition," or other explicit references to a TurboTax free product. The only relevant-looking item is the navigation link text "Free Edition tax filing," which is just a footer navigation label linking to the Free Edition site, not a promotional mention of "Turbotax free" on this page itself. The rule’s trigger is specific to when "Turbotax free" is mentioned in the content; that does not occur here. Therefore the rule is not triggered and is not applicable to this page.

Trigger: The page explicitly mentions the TurboTax Free product via the “Free Edition tax filing” link, which satisfies “Turbotax free is mentioned.” Requirement: The mandated disclosure (“~37% of filers qualify. Simple Form 1040 returns only…”) does not appear right underneath this mention (or anywhere nearby), so Axis A is not met. Axis B: No disclosure text is present, so it cannot match the approved library entry. Strongest counter-reading: One could argue a footer navigation link isn’t promotional content and thus shouldn’t trigger; however, the rule text is absolute (“If Turbotax free is mentioned”), and this is a clear product mention by name, so the trigger applies. Mentions like “100% free to start” were not treated as triggers since they refer to pricing for Full Service rather than the TurboTax Free Edition product; even if considered, there is still no required disclosure beneath them.
